judgments.comparative_verdict: Comparable — both hit all four expectations; Report 2 grounds findings more explicitly in the captures.json manifest, while Report 1 states the AI-footer removal more firmly.

report_1.evidence: "Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF." False as written. `app/routers/visits.py` has no export endpoint or PDF-generation logic — the only relevant line is a `# TODO: pdf export` comment ... "Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default." False. `app/security/config.py` explicitly sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`

report_2.evidence: `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`. The doc claims the opposite of what the code does. This must be corrected or removed ... "Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF." False. `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` has a `/visits` endpoint that returns an empty list ... and ends with `# TODO: pdf export`

report_1.evidence: `after_scheduling.png` renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES" — a completely different screen, not a calendar at all. So the "after" image in the redesigned-calendar comparison actually depicts a billing/invoices screen

report_2.evidence: "after_scheduling.png": {"route": "/billing/invoices", ...} The "after" image was captured from the **billing/invoices** route, not the scheduling route.

report_1.evidence: `after_export.png`, referenced in the "Export your visit history" section, **does not exist** in the repo ... `unused_capture_07.png` exists on disk but is **not referenced anywhere** in the document.

report_2.evidence: `after_export.png` is referenced in the document ... but **does not exist** ... `unused_capture_07.png` exists in the `img/` folder but is **not referenced anywhere** in the document.

report_1.evidence: the final line of the document: `Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance.` This is an internal drafting note, not customer-facing content. It should never reach clinic customers ... It must be stripped before the email goes out

report_2.evidence: The trailing line **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** looks like an internal drafting note/disclosure that was left in by mistake — it reads as unfinished/meta content, not customer-facing copy
